title: Global CSP permits inline scripts and eval on the landing route
reasoning: The /:locale landing page receives the global headers, including a script-src policy that allows both inline scripts and eval-like execution. That materially weakens the CSP for a public unauthenticated route: any future HTML/script injection on this route or shared layout is much easier to turn into arbitrary script execution, and 'unsafe-eval' also permits string-to-code sinks that CSP would otherwise block. This is not justified by the landing page code shown here, which does not require inline script execution or eval.
reproduction: Load /en and inspect the Content-Security-Policy response header; it includes script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval'.
recommendation: Remove 'unsafe-eval' from production CSP and replace 'unsafe-inline' with nonces or hashes for any required framework/runtime inline scripts. If a dependency requires eval in development, gate the relaxed directive behind a development-only config branch.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are linked for this route, and typical component/render tests would not assert response security headers.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a route/header test that requests a localized landing page and asserts the production CSP script-src does not include 'unsafe-inline' or 'unsafe-eval'.
